args.content: Recovered brownfield baseline.

documentation.value: Describe the element as a goal: a high-level end state or outcome that stakeholders intend to achieve. In this architecture, Product Delivery Goal defines the intended strategic outcome for Current Product Goal and anchors the rest of the baseline intention model. Recovered brownfield baseline.

Describe the element as a capability: an ability that an organization, person, or system possesses to achieve an outcome. In this architecture, Core Product Capability captures the organization or product's capacity to achieve Current Product Goal through coordinated business and application behavior.

Describe the element as a course of action: an approach, directive, or plan for configuring capabilities and resources. In this architecture, MVP Delivery Course Of Action prescribes a runnable local MVP with explicit architecture-to-implementation traceability as the preferred means of delivery.

Describe the element as a business actor: an organizational entity that is capable of performing behavior and owning or using business roles, processes, or services. In this architecture, Primary User identifies the main human or organizational party that consumes the delivered business service and interacts with the core business flow.

Describe the element as a business process: a sequence of business behavior that produces a defined set of business outcomes. In this architecture, Core Business Flow sequences the core activities needed to operationalize Current Product Goal and deliver the intended value to the primary user.

Describe the element as a business service: externally visible business behavior delivered to customers or other business roles. In this architecture, Core User Service is the user-facing service delivered through the core business process to the primary actor.

Describe the element as an application component: a modular application building block that encapsulates automated behavior and data. In this architecture, Core Application System is the modular software building block that automates and supports the business process behind Current Product Goal.

Describe the element as an application service: externally visible automated behavior provided by an application component. In this architecture, Core Application Service provides the externally visible automation boundary that supports and serves the core business service.

Describe the element as a data object: passive data suitable for automated processing by application behavior. In this architecture, Core Domain Data captures the domain and state information created, read, and maintained by the core application system.

Describe the element as a node: a computational or physical resource that hosts, executes, or supports technology and application behavior. In this architecture, Core Technology Runtime is the runtime environment that hosts and executes the technology and application behavior required for Current Product Goal.

Describe the element as an artifact: a physical or deployable piece of information used or produced in technology or application realization. In this architecture, Deployable Solution Artifact is the deployable solution package generated from the core application system for operation on the target runtime node.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Product Capability makes Product Delivery Goal concrete by providing the ability needed to achieve that strategic outcome.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, MVP Delivery Course Of Action gives concrete form to Core Product Capability by prescribing how that capability will be established.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Business Flow operationalizes Core Product Capability through executable business behavior.

Describe the relationship as an assignment: the allocation of responsibility, behavior, or execution from an active structure element to a behavior element, or vice versa where appropriate in ArchiMate. In this architecture, Primary User is allocated participation responsibility in Core Business Flow from a business perspective.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Business Flow implements and makes Core User Service concrete as externally visible behavior.

Describe the relationship as a serving relationship: the source concept provides functionality or value that is used by the target concept. In this architecture, Core User Service provides business value and externally visible behavior to Primary User.

Describe the relationship as a serving relationship: the source concept provides functionality or value that is used by the target concept. In this architecture, Core Application Service provides the automated support needed by the business-facing Core User Service.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Application System implements and makes Core Application Service concrete as the exposed application service boundary.

Describe the relationship as an access relationship: the source behavior or active structure concept reads, writes, or otherwise uses the target passive structure concept. In this architecture, Core Application System reads and writes Core Domain Data in order to execute application behavior.

Describe the relationship as an association: a generic structural or semantic link used when no more specific ArchiMate relationship type applies. In this architecture, Core Technology Runtime has a structural support link with Core Application System where no more specific baseline relationship is modeled.

Describe the relationship as a serving relationship: the source concept provides functionality or value that is used by the target concept. In this architecture, Core Technology Runtime provides the infrastructure behavior required to host and expose Core Application Service.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Deployable Solution Artifact is the concrete deployable manifestation of Core Application System.
Core layered intention model maintained by SystemArchitect.

args.title: Extension Background Orchestrator
args.content: Chrome extension MV3 background service worker that owns persisted assistant state, validates rule and permission preconditions, coordinates page capture, starts runs through the Python adapter, and routes runtime messages between sidepanel and content script.

documentation.value: Chrome extension MV3 background service worker that owns persisted assistant state, validates rule and permission preconditions, coordinates page capture, starts runs through the Python adapter, and routes runtime messages between sidepanel and content script.

args.title: Extension Content Capture Agent
args.content: Injected content-script component that extracts configured page fields and username context from the active DOM, renders the floating AI launcher, and toggles the embedded fallback panel inside accessible pages.

documentation.value: Injected content-script component that extracts configured page fields and username context from the active DOM, renders the floating AI launcher, and toggles the embedded fallback panel inside accessible pages.

args.title: Extension Sidepanel Conversation Shell
args.content: React sidepanel component that presents the conversation workspace, streams normalized run events, manages session history and rule drawers, and coordinates user actions such as send, retry, answer, recapture, and diagnostics export.

documentation.value: React sidepanel component that presents the conversation workspace, streams normalized run events, manages session history and rule drawers, and coordinates user actions such as send, retry, answer, recapture, and diagnostics export.

args.title: Extension Shared Contracts And Policy
args.content: Shared browser-side library that defines API contracts, runtime protocol types, manifest/config validation, rule matching, permission evaluation, and common state helpers reused by background, sidepanel, and content modules.

documentation.value: Shared browser-side library that defines API contracts, runtime protocol types, manifest/config validation, rule matching, permission evaluation, and common state helpers reused by background, sidepanel, and content modules.

args.title: Python Adapter Runtime Bridge
args.content: FastAPI adapter that exposes run, event-stream, answer, health, and feedback endpoints; discovers and enforces the remote opencode agent; creates or reuses sessions; normalizes upstream SSE traffic into extension-facing events; and journals invocation activity.

documentation.value: FastAPI adapter that exposes run, event-stream, answer, health, and feedback endpoints; discovers and enforces the remote opencode agent; creates or reuses sessions; normalizes upstream SSE traffic into extension-facing events; and journals invocation activity.

args.title: Backend Feedback Service
args.content: Express backend service providing HTTP boundaries for mock page analysis and message feedback acceptance, including CORS allowlisting, API-key enforcement, schema validation, timeout handling, and normalized error responses.

documentation.value: Express backend service providing HTTP boundaries for mock page analysis and message feedback acceptance, including CORS allowlisting, API-key enforcement, schema validation, timeout handling, and normalized error responses.

args.title: Local Test Fixture Site
args.content: Repo-local HTTP fixture that serves deterministic DOM elements, metadata, and page globals used to validate page capture, username extraction, permission flows, and end-to-end extension behavior during manual testing.

documentation.value: Repo-local HTTP fixture that serves deterministic DOM elements, metadata, and page globals used to validate page capture, username extraction, permission flows, and end-to-end extension behavior during manual testing.

args.title: Validate Active Tab Access
args.content:
[Input]: Active tab URL, configured optional host permissions, stored page rules, and current Chrome runtime permission state.
[Processing]: Evaluate whether the active page is restricted, whether any enabled rule matches the URL, and whether the domain has been granted host permission before allowing capture or run actions to proceed.
[Output]: An active-tab context or a normalized domain error explaining restricted page, missing rule match, or missing permission.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Restricted Chrome pages are rejected; unmatched pages return an explicit rule error; ungranted domains return an explicit permission error; allowed pages produce a context marked ready for capture.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Active tab URL, configured optional host permissions, stored page rules, and current Chrome runtime permission state.
[Processing]: Evaluate whether the active page is restricted, whether any enabled rule matches the URL, and whether the domain has been granted host permission before allowing capture or run actions to proceed.
[Output]: An active-tab context or a normalized domain error explaining restricted page, missing rule match, or missing permission.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Restricted Chrome pages are rejected; unmatched pages return an explicit rule error; ungranted domains return an explicit permission error; allowed pages produce a context marked ready for capture.

args.title: Capture Page Context
args.content:
[Input]: Active tab identifier, matched page rule field definitions, and content-script runtime channel.
[Processing]: Ensure the content script is ready, request configured field extraction from the page, resolve username context from the DOM or page globals, and persist captured state for later run startup.
[Output]: Captured field map plus username context, or a capture error when the content script cannot supply data.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Capture only runs on allowed tabs; enabled rule fields are requested from the page; missing responses surface a capture error; successful capture updates persisted extension state with latest page data.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Active tab identifier, matched page rule field definitions, and content-script runtime channel.
[Processing]: Ensure the content script is ready, request configured field extraction from the page, resolve username context from the DOM or page globals, and persist captured state for later run startup.
[Output]: Captured field map plus username context, or a capture error when the content script cannot supply data.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Capture only runs on allowed tabs; enabled rule fields are requested from the page; missing responses surface a capture error; successful capture updates persisted extension state with latest page data.

args.title: Start And Track Run
args.content:
[Input]: User prompt, selected main agent, optional session id, optional capture-page flag, existing assistant state, and Python adapter start-run API.
[Processing]: Optionally capture page data, select or reuse a session, call the adapter to create a run, initialize stream state, store run metadata, and service background messages for run lifecycle updates.
[Output]: A start-run response containing run/session metadata on success, or a normalized analysis error persisted into extension state on failure.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Prompted runs create or reuse the correct session; capture is only performed when requested; adapter failures are converted into domain errors; state reflects streaming status immediately after a successful start.

documentation.value:
[Input]: User prompt, selected main agent, optional session id, optional capture-page flag, existing assistant state, and Python adapter start-run API.
[Processing]: Optionally capture page data, select or reuse a session, call the adapter to create a run, initialize stream state, store run metadata, and service background messages for run lifecycle updates.
[Output]: A start-run response containing run/session metadata on success, or a normalized analysis error persisted into extension state on failure.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Prompted runs create or reuse the correct session; capture is only performed when requested; adapter failures are converted into domain errors; state reflects streaming status immediately after a successful start.

args.title: Extract Configured Page Fields
args.content:
[Input]: Browser document/window objects and a list of enabled field extraction rules using title, URL, selection, meta, selector text, or selector attribute sources.
[Processing]: Iterate through enabled field definitions, read each configured source from the live DOM, normalize values, and apply fallback values when direct extraction is empty.
[Output]: A key-value capture payload matching the requested field definitions.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Disabled fields are skipped; supported source types return trimmed values; empty direct values fall back correctly; returned keys align exactly with requested rule keys.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Browser document/window objects and a list of enabled field extraction rules using title, URL, selection, meta, selector text, or selector attribute sources.
[Processing]: Iterate through enabled field definitions, read each configured source from the live DOM, normalize values, and apply fallback values when direct extraction is empty.
[Output]: A key-value capture payload matching the requested field definitions.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Disabled fields are skipped; supported source types return trimmed values; empty direct values fall back correctly; returned keys align exactly with requested rule keys.

args.title: Resolve Username Context
args.content:
[Input]: Browser document/window objects containing possible username hints in DOM attributes, DOM text, meta tags, or page globals.
[Processing]: Search known username selectors first, then metadata, then well-known page-global objects, and record both the resolved username and the evidence source used.
[Output]: A username context object with username and usernameSource, defaulting to unknown fallback states when no evidence is found.
[Acceptance Criteria]: DOM data attributes outrank text and globals; meta and page-global fallbacks are supported; unresolved pages still return a structured username context; output always includes a source classification.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Browser document/window objects containing possible username hints in DOM attributes, DOM text, meta tags, or page globals.
[Processing]: Search known username selectors first, then metadata, then well-known page-global objects, and record both the resolved username and the evidence source used.
[Output]: A username context object with username and usernameSource, defaulting to unknown fallback states when no evidence is found.
[Acceptance Criteria]: DOM data attributes outrank text and globals; meta and page-global fallbacks are supported; unresolved pages still return a structured username context; output always includes a source classification.

args.title: Inject Launcher And Embedded Panel
args.content:
[Input]: Accessible page document/body state plus runtime messages requesting panel open or embedded fallback toggle.
[Processing]: Inject a floating AI button once per page, send OPEN_PANEL events when clicked, and create or remove an embedded iframe panel when fallback UI is requested.
[Output]: Visible launcher and panel side effects in the host page and ready responses for runtime ping/toggle messages.
[Acceptance Criteria]: The floating button is injected at most once; OPEN_PANEL is emitted on click; embedded panel toggles on repeated requests; no injection occurs before the document body is available.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Accessible page document/body state plus runtime messages requesting panel open or embedded fallback toggle.
[Processing]: Inject a floating AI button once per page, send OPEN_PANEL events when clicked, and create or remove an embedded iframe panel when fallback UI is requested.
[Output]: Visible launcher and panel side effects in the host page and ready responses for runtime ping/toggle messages.
[Acceptance Criteria]: The floating button is injected at most once; OPEN_PANEL is emitted on click; embedded panel toggles on repeated requests; no injection occurs before the document body is available.

args.title: Render Transcript And Stage
args.content:
[Input]: Current assistant state, historical run details, live normalized run events, answers, and selected session context.
[Processing]: Project stable transcript parts from historical and live runs, derive presentation status models, and render the main conversation stage with summary, reasoning, result, and error views.
[Output]: A sidepanel read model and rendered conversation shell reflecting the selected or active session.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Historical and live segments are merged without losing ordering; selected-session switching changes the rendered thread; terminal output and errors remain visible after streaming; empty sessions render an explicit no-content state.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Current assistant state, historical run details, live normalized run events, answers, and selected session context.
[Processing]: Project stable transcript parts from historical and live runs, derive presentation status models, and render the main conversation stage with summary, reasoning, result, and error views.
[Output]: A sidepanel read model and rendered conversation shell reflecting the selected or active session.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Historical and live segments are merged without losing ordering; selected-session switching changes the rendered thread; terminal output and errors remain visible after streaming; empty sessions render an explicit no-content state.

args.title: Manage Sessions, Rules, And Permissions
args.content:
[Input]: Stored rules, selected session key, active-tab context, permission APIs, and user actions from drawers and panels.
[Processing]: Load and edit rules, switch sessions, request host permissions for the current domain, refresh context and history, and coordinate drawer state for session, context, rules, and run workspaces.
[Output]: Updated rule set, selected-session state, refreshed active context, and permission feedback surfaced in the sidepanel.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Rule create, update, and delete flows persist through background messaging; session selection remains stable across refreshes; permission requests surface success or actionable failure messages; drawer state reflects the active workspace.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Stored rules, selected session key, active-tab context, permission APIs, and user actions from drawers and panels.
[Processing]: Load and edit rules, switch sessions, request host permissions for the current domain, refresh context and history, and coordinate drawer state for session, context, rules, and run workspaces.
[Output]: Updated rule set, selected-session state, refreshed active context, and permission feedback surfaced in the sidepanel.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Rule create, update, and delete flows persist through background messaging; session selection remains stable across refreshes; permission requests surface success or actionable failure messages; drawer state reflects the active workspace.

args.title: Drive Run Streaming And Answers
args.content:
[Input]: User prompt, selected agent, selected session target, SSE event stream from the adapter, optional retry metadata, and question-answer submissions.
[Processing]: Start runs through background messaging, open the SSE stream, merge incoming normalized events into local state and history, handle question submissions, and export diagnostics for live or historical runs.
[Output]: Streaming sidepanel state, persisted run history, persisted events and answers, and diagnostic export artifacts when requested.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Successful run start transitions the UI to connecting and streaming; incoming events update run status and final output incrementally; question answers clear pending-question state and persist answer records; diagnostics export includes either live or historical run evidence.

documentation.value:
[Input]: User prompt, selected agent, selected session target, SSE event stream from the adapter, optional retry metadata, and question-answer submissions.
[Processing]: Start runs through background messaging, open the SSE stream, merge incoming normalized events into local state and history, handle question submissions, and export diagnostics for live or historical runs.
[Output]: Streaming sidepanel state, persisted run history, persisted events and answers, and diagnostic export artifacts when requested.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Successful run start transitions the UI to connecting and streaming; incoming events update run status and final output incrementally; question answers clear pending-question state and persist answer records; diagnostics export includes either live or historical run evidence.

args.title: Validate Extension Configuration
args.content:
[Input]: Raw build-time environment variables, requested extension mode, allowed API origins, host permission patterns, and web-accessible resource matches.
[Processing]: Parse environment values, validate HTTPS or local-development exceptions, constrain controlled host patterns, derive manifest settings, and reject overly broad or inconsistent configuration.
[Output]: A normalized extension configuration and manifest-compatible permission model, or validation errors for unsafe configuration.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Non-local HTTP APIs are rejected outside development; API base URL must belong to the allowlist; host patterns cannot use unsupported broad forms; web-accessible matches stay within controlled host permissions.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Raw build-time environment variables, requested extension mode, allowed API origins, host permission patterns, and web-accessible resource matches.
[Processing]: Parse environment values, validate HTTPS or local-development exceptions, constrain controlled host patterns, derive manifest settings, and reject overly broad or inconsistent configuration.
[Output]: A normalized extension configuration and manifest-compatible permission model, or validation errors for unsafe configuration.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Non-local HTTP APIs are rejected outside development; API base URL must belong to the allowlist; host patterns cannot use unsupported broad forms; web-accessible matches stay within controlled host permissions.

args.title: Call Adapter And Parse Event Contracts
args.content:
[Input]: Run-start, answer, feedback, and stream requests plus API base URL, optional API key, and expected response schemas.
[Processing]: Build HTTP requests to the Python adapter, open EventSource streams for run events, validate JSON responses with zod schemas, and convert transport failures into domain errors.
[Output]: Typed start-run, answer, feedback, or stream-event results consumable by background and sidepanel modules.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Requests include configured headers and auth when present; response payloads are schema-validated before use; invalid or failed network responses become structured domain errors; SSE events are parsed into normalized run-event objects.

name.value: Call Adapter And Parse Event Contracts

documentation.value:
[Input]: Run-start, answer, feedback, and stream requests plus API base URL, optional API key, and expected response schemas.
[Processing]: Build HTTP requests to the Python adapter, open EventSource streams for run events, validate JSON responses with zod schemas, and convert transport failures into domain errors.
[Output]: Typed start-run, answer, feedback, or stream-event results consumable by background and sidepanel modules.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Requests include configured headers and auth when present; response payloads are schema-validated before use; invalid or failed network responses become structured domain errors; SSE events are parsed into normalized run-event objects.

args.title: Match Rules And Canonicalize Capture
args.content:
[Input]: Current page URL, stored page rules, configured field definitions, and raw captured field values.
[Processing]: Convert wildcard hostname and path patterns to matchers, identify the first enabled rule matching a page, capture requested fields, and transform raw values into canonical pageTitle, pageUrl, metaDescription, h1, selectedText, software_version, and selected_sr outputs.
[Output]: Matching rule metadata and canonical captured fields used by background and sidepanel flows.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Disabled rules never match; wildcard host and path matching is case-insensitive; canonical capture always returns the expected standard keys; software_version and selected_sr are preserved when present.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Current page URL, stored page rules, configured field definitions, and raw captured field values.
[Processing]: Convert wildcard hostname and path patterns to matchers, identify the first enabled rule matching a page, capture requested fields, and transform raw values into canonical pageTitle, pageUrl, metaDescription, h1, selectedText, software_version, and selected_sr outputs.
[Output]: Matching rule metadata and canonical captured fields used by background and sidepanel flows.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Disabled rules never match; wildcard host and path matching is case-insensitive; canonical capture always returns the expected standard keys; software_version and selected_sr are preserved when present.

args.title: Start Adapter Run And Session
args.content:
[Input]: RunStartRequest with prompt, selectedAgent, optional capture or sessionId, username context, adapter settings, and remote opencode endpoints.
[Processing]: Validate the requested main agent, discover the canonical remote agent from /agent, create or reuse a session, dispatch prompt_async, record run state, and map startup failures into HTTP errors.
[Output]: Extension-facing run metadata containing runId, selectedAgent, optional sessionId, and persisted startup evidence for later streaming.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Unsupported agents are rejected; /agent discovery must yield exactly one allowed canonical remote agent; session reuse honors incoming sessionId; startup errors are surfaced as deterministic HTTP responses.

documentation.value:
[Input]: RunStartRequest with prompt, selectedAgent, optional capture or sessionId, username context, adapter settings, and remote opencode endpoints.
[Processing]: Validate the requested main agent, discover the canonical remote agent from /agent, create or reuse a session, dispatch prompt_async, record run state, and map startup failures into HTTP errors.
[Output]: Extension-facing run metadata containing runId, selectedAgent, optional sessionId, and persisted startup evidence for later streaming.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Unsupported agents are rejected; /agent discovery must yield exactly one allowed canonical remote agent; session reuse honors incoming sessionId; startup errors are surfaced as deterministic HTTP responses.

args.title: Normalize Opencode Events To SSE
args.content:
[Input]: Global opencode SSE payloads, session message payloads, question replies, run state, and semantic metadata rules for text, reasoning, and tool emissions.
[Processing]: Filter events to the active session, classify message deltas and part updates, enforce canonical-agent evidence, convert upstream events into normalized thinking, tool_call, question, result, and error records, and stream them to the extension while journaling logs.
[Output]: A normalized extension-facing SSE message stream with stable identities, semantic metadata, and terminal result or error events.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Only events for the run session are emitted; assistant text, reasoning, and tool updates are semantically distinguished; agent mismatch raises an explicit runtime error; a final result is emitted from session messages when available.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Global opencode SSE payloads, session message payloads, question replies, run state, and semantic metadata rules for text, reasoning, and tool emissions.
[Processing]: Filter events to the active session, classify message deltas and part updates, enforce canonical-agent evidence, convert upstream events into normalized thinking, tool_call, question, result, and error records, and stream them to the extension while journaling logs.
[Output]: A normalized extension-facing SSE message stream with stable identities, semantic metadata, and terminal result or error events.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Only events for the run session are emitted; assistant text, reasoning, and tool updates are semantically distinguished; agent mismatch raises an explicit runtime error; a final result is emitted from session messages when available.

args.title: Relay Feedback And Invocation Logs
args.content:
[Input]: MessageFeedbackRequest, adapter API-key settings, backend feedback endpoint, and invocation-log storage path.
[Processing]: Validate caller auth, forward feedback to the backend service, validate backend response shape, write structured JSONL invocation records for run starts, streamed events, answers, and feedback, and return normalized API responses.
[Output]: Accepted feedback responses plus append-only invocation log records on disk.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Backend calls include the configured API key when present; malformed or failing backend responses are translated into HTTP exceptions; successful feedback returns accepted=true with run and message identifiers; invocation log entries are appended as JSONL without blocking API flow.

documentation.value:
[Input]: MessageFeedbackRequest, adapter API-key settings, backend feedback endpoint, and invocation-log storage path.
[Processing]: Validate caller auth, forward feedback to the backend service, validate backend response shape, write structured JSONL invocation records for run starts, streamed events, answers, and feedback, and return normalized API responses.
[Output]: Accepted feedback responses plus append-only invocation log records on disk.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Backend calls include the configured API key when present; malformed or failing backend responses are translated into HTTP exceptions; successful feedback returns accepted=true with run and message identifiers; invocation log entries are appended as JSONL without blocking API flow.

args.title: Analyze Captured Page Payload
args.content:
[Input]: Analyze request payload containing canonical capture fields, optional context metadata, configured provider, and analysis timeout.
[Processing]: Validate the request body with zod, optionally enforce API-key auth, call the configured analysis provider under timeout control, and wrap provider output into the backend response contract.
[Output]: An ok=true analyze response containing markdown analysis, provider name, and duration metadata.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Invalid payloads produce validation errors; auth is enforced when API_KEY is configured; provider execution respects timeout limits; successful responses return the provider result under the documented data envelope.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Analyze request payload containing canonical capture fields, optional context metadata, configured provider, and analysis timeout.
[Processing]: Validate the request body with zod, optionally enforce API-key auth, call the configured analysis provider under timeout control, and wrap provider output into the backend response contract.
[Output]: An ok=true analyze response containing markdown analysis, provider name, and duration metadata.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Invalid payloads produce validation errors; auth is enforced when API_KEY is configured; provider execution respects timeout limits; successful responses return the provider result under the documented data envelope.

args.title: Accept Message Feedback
args.content:
[Input]: Message feedback payload with runId, messageId, and like or dislike value plus optional API-key credentials.
[Processing]: Authenticate the caller when configured, validate feedback payload shape, and return an acceptance envelope with echoed identifiers and updated timestamp.
[Output]: A normalized feedback response confirming acceptance of the requested feedback mutation.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Missing or invalid credentials are rejected when auth is enabled; only like and dislike feedback values are accepted; echoed run and message identifiers match the request; accepted responses include updatedAt in ISO timestamp form.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Message feedback payload with runId, messageId, and like or dislike value plus optional API-key credentials.
[Processing]: Authenticate the caller when configured, validate feedback payload shape, and return an acceptance envelope with echoed identifiers and updated timestamp.
[Output]: A normalized feedback response confirming acceptance of the requested feedback mutation.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Missing or invalid credentials are rejected when auth is enabled; only like and dislike feedback values are accepted; echoed run and message identifiers match the request; accepted responses include updatedAt in ISO timestamp form.

args.title: Enforce Backend HTTP Boundary
args.content:
[Input]: Incoming HTTP origin, request headers and body, backend environment allowlists, and raised runtime or validation exceptions.
[Processing]: Apply CORS allowlisting, parse JSON bodies, map zod and domain errors into uniform response envelopes, and shield callers from unhandled server exceptions via fallback analysis errors.
[Output]: Consistent HTTP success or error responses for backend analyze and feedback endpoints.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Disallowed origins are rejected; JSON requests are parsed within configured limits; domain and validation errors return structured error codes and messages; unknown exceptions are converted to a fallback analysis error response.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Incoming HTTP origin, request headers and body, backend environment allowlists, and raised runtime or validation exceptions.
[Processing]: Apply CORS allowlisting, parse JSON bodies, map zod and domain errors into uniform response envelopes, and shield callers from unhandled server exceptions via fallback analysis errors.
[Output]: Consistent HTTP success or error responses for backend analyze and feedback endpoints.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Disallowed origins are rejected; JSON requests are parsed within configured limits; domain and validation errors return structured error codes and messages; unknown exceptions are converted to a fallback analysis error response.

args.title: Serve Local Test Fixture
args.content:
[Input]: Local filesystem path to the test_site directory plus a fixed host and port binding of 127.0.0.1:4173.
[Processing]: Create a threaded HTTP server rooted at the test_site directory and serve static fixture content for manual extension testing.
[Output]: A reachable local HTTP endpoint exposing the repo-local fixture pages.
[Acceptance Criteria]: The server binds to 127.0.0.1:4173; files are served from the test_site directory; requests are handled concurrently by the threaded server; startup prints the local access URL.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Local filesystem path to the test_site directory plus a fixed host and port binding of 127.0.0.1:4173.
[Processing]: Create a threaded HTTP server rooted at the test_site directory and serve static fixture content for manual extension testing.
[Output]: A reachable local HTTP endpoint exposing the repo-local fixture pages.
[Acceptance Criteria]: The server binds to 127.0.0.1:4173; files are served from the test_site directory; requests are handled concurrently by the threaded server; startup prints the local access URL.

args.title: Expose Deterministic Capture Probes
args.content:
[Input]: Static HTML fixture markup consumed by the extension content script and rule engine.
[Processing]: Publish deterministic DOM selectors, logged-in-user metadata, data attributes, and page-global username objects that mirror the extension's capture and username-resolution expectations.
[Output]: A predictable browser page containing pageTitle, meta user tag, data-software-version, data-selected-sr, data-username, and window.__CURRENT_USER__ evidence.
[Acceptance Criteria]: The fixture includes stable selectors for software_version and selected_sr; username evidence exists in both DOM and page-global forms; metadata and title are available for capture; fixture content remains deterministic for repeatable manual validation.

documentation.value:
[Input]: Static HTML fixture markup consumed by the extension content script and rule engine.
[Processing]: Publish deterministic DOM selectors, logged-in-user metadata, data attributes, and page-global username objects that mirror the extension's capture and username-resolution expectations.
[Output]: A predictable browser page containing pageTitle, meta user tag, data-software-version, data-selected-sr, data-username, and window.__CURRENT_USER__ evidence.
[Acceptance Criteria]: The fixture includes stable selectors for software_version and selected_sr; username evidence exists in both DOM and page-global forms; metadata and title are available for capture; fixture content remains deterministic for repeatable manual validation.

args.title: Background orchestrator performs active-tab access validation
args.content: Recovered brownfield assignment between the background component and its access-gating function.

name.value: Background orchestrator performs active-tab access validation

documentation.value: Recovered brownfield assignment between the background component and its access-gating function.

args.title: Background orchestrator performs page capture
